steps.title: Use a common denominator
steps.content:
The first term already has denominator $k$. Rewrite the second term over the same denominator by multiplying by 
$$
\frac{k}{k}
$$
$$
36ak=\frac{36ak^2}{k}
$$

steps.title: Combine the fractions
steps.content:
Now both terms are written over $k$, so add the numerators.
$$
\frac{36a}{k}+\frac{36ak^2}{k}=\frac{36a+36ak^2}{k}
$$

steps.title: Factor the numerator
steps.content: The numerator has a common factor of $36a$. $$ \frac{36a+36ak^2}{k}=\frac{36a(k^2+1)}{k} $$ The answer is D.
